title: gardenia rutenbergiana subsp. australis
intro: Gardenia rutenbergiana subsp. australis, also known as the ' Natal gardenia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a subtropical shrub native to the southeastern coast of Africa, prized for its white, highly fragrant flowers and glossy green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of the Natal gardenia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies, but it is essential to note that its use should be consulted with a medical professional for safety and efficacy.
